notes: Pickleheads lists 5 locations / 23 courts for Los Gatos (site:pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/los-gatos, confirmed live via WebSearch; direct WebFetch to pickleheads.com returns HTTP 403 as documented elsewhere in this repo). Only 4 of the 5 could be identified by name despite extensive cross-referencing: Bay Club Courtside (8 courts), Addison-Penzak JCC / 'Jewish Community Center' on Pickleheads (4 courts per Pickleheads, 6 per the venue's own site), La Rinconada Park (4 courts), and Raymond J. Fisher Middle School (3 courts) — summing to 19-21 of the stated 23. Pickleheads' own aggregate stat breakdown for the city ('2 locations with concrete', '1 hard', '1 asphalt', '1 wood' = 5 surface types matching 5 locations) implies a 5th, outdoor, ~4-court venue with an ASPHALT surface, distinct from all 4 confirmed venues (whose surfaces are concrete/concrete/hard/wood respectively) — but no independent source (Bounce, Pickleballify, Pickleballrush, PicklePlay, tennispickleballcalifornia.com, GoodRun, Places2Play, Yelp, or the Town's own records) names a 5th public pickleball location, and Pickleheads itself blocks direct fetches so its own venue list couldn't be enumerated beyond what WebSearch snippets surfaced. Ruled out as candidates during this search: Los Gatos Swim and Racquet Club (confirmed tennis-only via its own site, no pickleball), La Rinconada Country Club (private golf club adjacent to but distinct from La Rinconada Park; no pickleball evidence), Vasona Lake County Park (no pickleball found), Blossom Hill Park (Town's own Sept 2023 Parks Commission report confirms this is tennis-only; a pickleball conversion was proposed there in that report but never funded — see la-rinconada-park notes), Pacific Courts (a Los Gatos-based court-construction contractor, not a playable public venue), and several Swimply listings for private backyard courts (excluded as non-public per this site's standard). Left as a genuine, unresolved gap rather than guessed at. A human should either get direct (non-blocked) access to pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/los-gatos to identify the 5th venue by name, or treat Pickleheads' 23-court total as possibly stale/inflated. Also explicitly out of scope and excluded from this file: El Quito Park and Redwood Middle School, both real pickleball venues actively promoted on the Los Gatos-Saratoga Recreation Department's own pickleball page (lgsrecreation.org/pickleball/, which loosely parenthesizes El Quito Park as '(Los Gatos)') but which are geographically located in the separate, neighboring city of Saratoga, CA (confirmed via saratoga.ca.us and Pickleheads' own /california/saratoga/ slug for El Quito Park) — not actually within Los Gatos town limits, so excluded per this task's scope even though the joint recreation department serves both towns. Notably, only one of the four confirmed venues (La Rinconada Park) is Town-owned and free; the Town's own 2023 Parks Commission report states La Rinconada 'has the only pickleball courts in Los Gatos' in that Town-owned/free sense. La Rinconada Park has been the subject of an active, well-documented noise dispute between neighbors and players (2022-2025), including vandalism in 2025 and a $90,000 Town-funded renovation (resurfacing, sound barriers, security cameras, a time-sensitive locking gate) completed December 2025 — see that venue's notes for details, since this is important current-events context for anyone publishing a Los Gatos page. lat/lon and Google Maps cid for all 4 confirmed venues were independently verified live via Google Maps during this research (not carried forward from any existing record, since Los Gatos has zero existing entries in assets/courts-data.json / assets/venues.json).

venues.name: Bay Club Courtside
venues.address: 14675 S Winchester Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032
venues.surface: Outdoor concrete, dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.price: Membership required (Bay Club multi-sport/social membership); specific pickleball guest-pass or drop-in pricing is not published online
venues.hours: General club hours: Mon-Fri 5:30am-10pm, Sat-Sun 6am-9pm (pickleball-specific programmed hours not published separately)
venues.weather: Warm, sunny South Bay summers tempered by its foothill setting at the base of the Santa Cruz Mountains -- cooler evenings than the valley floor; mild, wetter winters
venues.notes: Court count (8 outdoor concrete, dedicated permanent lines/nets) is corroborated independently by Pickleheads and tennispickleballcalifornia.com's mirror with no contradicting figure found anywhere -- high confidence. Address appears as '14675 Winchester Blvd' (no direction) on Google Maps but as '14675 S Winchester Blvd' on Bay Club's own site and Yelp; used the venue's own site's form. Sits directly adjacent to Netflix's Los Gatos corporate campus (visible on Google Maps as 'Netflix - Building D/E/F/G' immediately north); unrelated, just a geographic landmark. No Pickleheads-specific star rating found (Google lists 4.2 stars/415 reviews, but that's a Google figure, not Pickleheads, so left out per this field's definition). lat/lon/cid independently confirmed via live Google Maps lookup during this research.

venues.name: Addison-Penzak JCC
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court (described elsewhere as concrete/acrylic), permanent lines
venues.price: Membership required for general access; non-members can buy a 10-visit pickleball punchcard for $100 (valid 6 months); clinics $30 ($25 members); workshops $180 ($150 members)
venues.hours: General facility hours: Mon-Fri 6am-9pm, Sat-Sun 7am-7pm. Pickleball open play: Tue 8-10am, Thu 4-6pm, Sat 4:30-6:30pm, Sun 9am-12pm
venues.skill: Beginner lessons and private coaching available; skill-level clinics and workshops offered
venues.weather: Warm, sunny South Bay summers tempered by its foothill setting at the base of the Santa Cruz Mountains -- cooler evenings than the valley floor; mild, wetter winters
venues.notes: Unresolved court-count conflict: Pickleheads and Bounce both independently state 4 outdoor courts; the venue's own official page (apjcc.org/fitness1/pickleball/) states 6 lighted pickleball courts. Pickleheads' sitemap shows this page was last modified 2022-10-07 -- plausible the JCC added courts since, especially given nationwide 2022-2026 growth in JCC pickleball programs, but this isn't confirmed, so courts is set to the venue's own (more current, first-party) figure of 6 and the conflict is flagged here rather than silently picking a side. Per Patch, this venue opened with just 2 outdoor courts in September 2015 ($8/day, free for members) and has evidently expanded since. Pickleheads/Bounce list this venue simply as 'Jewish Community Center'; its actual/formal name (own site, Google Maps, Yelp) is 'Addison-Penzak JCC' (of Silicon Valley), used here instead for clarity -- pickleheadsUrl slug retained as-is for traceability. Do not confuse with the separate, tennis-only Los Gatos Swim and Racquet Club two blocks away on the same Oka Rd corridor -- confirmed via that club's own site to have zero pickleball courts, so it is excluded from this file entirely. No Pickleheads-specific star rating found. lat/lon/cid independently confirmed via live Google Maps lookup during this research.

venues.name: La Rinconada Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court -- 4 pickleball courts striped directly onto a single former tennis court in fall 2018 (First Serve Productions striping project)
venues.netType: Bring your own net, on permanent painted lines
venues.hours: Town park hours are officially sunrise to one hour after sunset daily; Google Maps currently (checked July 2026) lists an 8 AM opening, plausibly reflecting the time-sensitive locking gate installed in the Town's Oct-Dec 2025 court renovation -- exact current posted hours were not independently confirmed from a Town source
venues.weather: Warm, sunny South Bay summers tempered by its foothill setting at the base of the Santa Cruz Mountains -- cooler evenings than the valley floor; mild, wetter winters
sources: Town of Los Gatos Parks Commission Report, 'Discuss and Provide Feedback on Pickleball at La Rinconada Park,' meeting date 10/02/2023 (PDF, mccmeetingspublic.blob.core.usgovcloudapi.net)
venues.notes: Best-documented venue in this file: the Town's own Sept 28, 2023 Parks Commission staff report (primary source) confirms the tennis court here was striped into 4 pickleball courts in fall 2018 as a pilot program, and explicitly calls La Rinconada 'the only pickleball courts in Los Gatos' in the Town-owned/free sense (i.e. not counting the private Bay Club Courtside and Addison-Penzak JCC, and predating Fisher Middle School's LGSR programming). No lights (Town's own page calls it 'unlit'); no restrooms on site (frequently flagged by reviewers); on-street parking only. IMPORTANT recent history: this park has been the site of a multi-year noise dispute between pickleball players and adjacent residents (documented back to 2022 Commission minutes); in 2025 two of the four courts were deliberately vandalized with oil, and the Town announced a $90,000 renovation (resurfacing, acoustic sound barriers, security cameras, a time-sensitive locking gate) that ran October-December 2025 and 'will consume most of the town's pickleball budget' per Local News Matters -- as of a live Aug 2025 San Jose Spotlight report, the Town explicitly said it 'isn't considering expanding pickleball amenities elsewhere' in Los Gatos. As of this research, La Rinconada currently has no LGSR programming and no individual reservations (fully open walk-up to the public), with LGSR programming expected to return around mid-2026 per a Bounce-sourced search snippet -- not independently verified on a Town page, flagged as lower-confidence. The 2023 report also floated (but never funded) converting one tennis court at Blossom Hill Park to 4 more pickleball courts; that did not happen and Blossom Hill remains tennis-only per that same report and no source found since suggests otherwise. No Pickleheads-specific star rating found (a 4.3/13-review figure exists on Chamber of Commerce data, not Pickleheads, so left out). lat/lon/cid independently confirmed via live Google Maps lookup during this research.

venues.name: Raymond J. Fisher Middle School
venues.surface: Indoor wood gymnasium court, permanent lines
venues.netType: Bring your own net, on permanent painted lines
venues.price: Fee required -- sources conflict on structure (see notes)
venues.hours: LGS Recreation weekend Open Play for club members: Sat/Sun 9am-3pm; no independently confirmed general-public hours
venues.notes: Two unresolved conflicts, both left for human review rather than guessed at. (1) Court count: Pickleheads and Bounce independently agree on 3 indoor wood courts (BYO net); Pickleballify instead states 'three indoor and three outdoor' courts (6 total) at this same venue, which looks like it may be conflating the school's separate outdoor blacktop/tennis courts with pickleball, but that can't be ruled out from available sources -- courts is set to the 2-source-agreed value of 3. (2) Fee structure: Pickleheads and Bounce both describe a flat 'one-time fee' to play; the Los Gatos-Saratoga Recreation Department's own program page instead describes access via a $100/year 'Adult Pickleball Club' annual membership (which also covers El Quito Park and other benefits -- El Quito Park itself is in Saratoga, not Los Gatos, and is excluded from this file, see top-level notes), with Fisher Middle School weekend Open Play (Sat/Sun 9am-3pm) restricted to members. It's unclear whether a true one-time/drop-in fee option exists separately from that annual membership, or whether aggregators are just simplifying the membership as a 'one-time fee.' As a school gym, access outside LGSR-scheduled windows is presumably otherwise restricted by school hours, though no source confirms this explicitly. No Pickleheads-specific star rating found. lat/lon/cid independently confirmed via live Google Maps lookup during this research.
